title: selago immersa
intro: Selago immersa, a plant species native to South Africa, is a type of succulent plant characterized by its fleshy leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Selago immersa typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of ointments or salves.
